title: RSA migration: cross-validate post-migration output
description:
After the Rust rewrite (wolfssl-rs-7 Rust rewrite issue), run full cross-validation to confirm no regressions and exact deterministic byte-match where possible.

Checks to run:
1. PKCS#1v1.5 exact byte match: re-run baseline test vectors from wolfssl-rs-siu against new native path; signature bytes must be identical
2. Wycheproof OAEP: re-run wolfssl-rs-573 vectors against new native path; all must pass
3. Wycheproof PKCS#1v1.5 verify: run against new path; all must pass (covers malformed signatures)
4. PSS round-trip: sign with new path, verify with new path; also verify with a reference (ring or RustCrypto rsa crate if available)
5. Key round-trip: generate key → export PKCS#1 DER → reimport → export again → bytes identical
6. Public key round-trip: export SPKI DER from private → reimport as public → encrypt/decrypt round-trip

Acceptance criteria:
- All 6 checks pass on both x86_64 (default build) and vendored build
- No test is allowed to pass by softening assertions — if bytes differ, investigate before proceeding
